name: Momentum
description: Trend-following model focused on range expansion and continuation after compression or pullback absorption.

context: Trade in the direction of the higher-timeframe impulse or fresh session expansion.
Mark the breakout base, recent swing, and liquidity target before entry.
Wait for price to hold outside the broken range instead of chasing the first spike.
setup: Compression or pullback forms near the breakout level.
Retest shows shallow rejection and no deep close back inside range.
Order flow stays one-sided with consecutive closes in trend direction.
trigger: Enter on break of the retest high for longs or retest low for shorts.
Allow first continuation-candle entry only if body closes beyond trigger level.
Skip entries if the trigger candle is already stretched more than one recent average range.
confirmation: Range expansion is above recent average.
Volume or speed increases through the trigger level.
Structure stays above reclaimed support for longs or below reclaimed resistance for shorts.
invalidation: Two-sided chop returns inside the prior range.
Retest closes back through the breakout base.
Session impulse fades and divergence becomes obvious.
noTrade: Major news hits within minutes of entry.
Breakout runs directly into higher-timeframe opposing structure.
Spread widens or the candle is mostly wick with no close-through.

stopLoss: Hide stop beyond retest low or high or behind the breakout base.
If structure is loose, use the far side of the compression box.
Cancel the setup if the stop needed is too wide for the plan.
takeProfit: Pay at the first extension or nearby liquidity.
Hold the remainder toward session high, low, or measured move only if continuation stays clean.
Do not widen target after entry.
timeExit: Flatten if continuation never appears within 3 to 6 execution candles.
Do not carry a scalp-style breakout into dead hours.
earlyExit: A full candle closes back inside range.
A counter impulse prints with larger body than the entry candle.
The breakout level fails on the next retest.
